title: Scope use: actions to element lifecycle
description: A use: action owns setup and teardown for one element and must return a destroy to release what it created.
body: Use when attaching imperative DOM behavior (tooltip, click-outside, intersection observer) via a use: action. Do not set up listeners or observers without a matching teardown. Actions: in the action, set up on the passed node, return an object with update (when params change) and destroy (to remove listeners/observers); keep the action focused on one element's behavior. Validation signals include a test that removing the element calls destroy and detaches the listener. Risk boundary: an action without destroy leaks observers/listeners as elements churn in lists; keep secret or global state out of per-element actions.
